compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
## Why

"Surface the same tools we use" includes visual authoring. The existing editor already enumerates
criteria (`prompt_library.enumerate_library`, packaged + `.rebar/prompts/`), authors criterion
prompts (`create_prompt(kind=criterion)` via the `/library/create` route), and edits routing in
workflow YAML — so authoring is incremental. The one genuinely net-new piece is a **live
must-fire/must-not-fire preview**: the editor has NO prompt-execution endpoint today (its `POST`
set is `/save`, `/prompt/save`, `/validate`, `/library/create`). This is a structurally
independent deliverable (its own endpoint + spike).

## What

- Extend the editor to author a project criterion end-to-end: prompt + a **routing-fields form**
  (exec, applies_at/applies_to, block_threshold, default_posture/blocking_enabled, fail_mode) that
  writes the `.rebar/` overlay + activation, reusing `create_prompt`/`update_prompt`.
- New **`POST /criterion/preview`** endpoint (synchronous): `{criterion_id | inline(prompt+routing),
  fixture}` → `{verdict: "fire"|"no-fire", finding?: <findings.py shape>, rationale: str}`,
  reusing the story `#3` per-criterion eval runner behind the `agents` extra + credentials, with a
  **configurable timeout (default 60s)**.
- **Spike gate (resolve before committing UI):** define the pass/fail — if a single sync preview
  does not return within the timeout on the reference model, fall back to an **async job+poll**
  shape (define the job id + poll endpoint) rather than blocking the editor's `ThreadingHTTPServer`
  handler. Do not leave the sync-vs-async decision unspecified.

## Scope

- Files: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor.py` (routes), `editor_assets/` (form + preview UI),
  `prompt_library.py` (authoring reuse).
- Depends on story `#1` (overlay/activation to author into) and story `#3` (the runner the preview
  reuses).
- Reuse: `enumerate_library`, `create_prompt`/`update_prompt`, the `/library/create` route.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The editor authors a project criterion: prompt + routing-fields form writing the `.rebar/`
      overlay + `activate` list (reusing `create_prompt`/`update_prompt`).
- [ ] `POST /criterion/preview` runs a live must-fire/must-not-fire preview with the defined
      request/response contract, configurable timeout (default 60s), reusing the eval runner.
- [ ] The spike gate is resolved: a defined pass/fail threshold and a concrete async job+poll
      fallback if sync execution is infeasible in the editor's HTTP handler.
- [ ] Authoring + preview work for both an LLM criterion and a DET invariant.


## Preview dispatch, entry point & atomic authoring (remediation, run 1)

### Preview entry point — reuse the LANDED runners (clears the reuse-claim blocker)
Both halves now exist on `main`:
- **LLM criterion** (`exec: 1-TURN`/`AGENT`): the preview runs it as its Pass-1 finder over the
  fixture text via `eval_solver.run_case(prompt_id, {"input": <fixture>}, runner=…, repo_root=…)`
  (the criterion arm added in story 55b8, which wraps `passes.pass1_chunk`). Fire ⇔ non-empty
  `findings`.
- **DET invariant** (`exec: DET`): the preview resolves the criterion's `detector` selector and runs
  the grounding scan the way `plan_review/det_invariants._run_one` does (story 7f0d) — a match ⇒
  fire, an abstain ⇒ reported per `fail_mode`. (The preview scans the fixture content, not a ticket
  `file_impact`, since it is an isolated must-fire/must-not-fire probe.)

### `POST /criterion/preview` dispatch (clears the LLM-vs-DET dispatch blocker)
The endpoint reads the criterion's `exec` DIRECTLY from its routing/descriptor (NOT `exec_tier`,
which has no DET arm — per 7f0d): `exec == "DET"` → the DET path above; else → the LLM path. Request
`{criterion_id | inline(prompt+routing), fixture:{input, expect?}}` → response `{verdict:
"fire"|"no-fire", finding?: <findings.py finding shape>, rationale}`. A criterion that needs a
graph/log (container/ISF) → a clear 4xx (not runnable as an inline preview). Synchronous with a
configurable timeout (default 60s); the spike-gate fallback (async job+poll) applies only if a
single sync preview exceeds the timeout on the reference model.

### Atomic dual-write for routing-fields authoring (clears G6 atomic blocker)
Authoring a project criterion touches TWO files: `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` (the rubric, via
`create_prompt` — already atomic) and the `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay routing entry.
Coordination: the criterion is only ACTIVE when it is in BOTH the routing map AND the `activate`
list (ef7e semantics). The write order is prompt-first, then a single atomic JSON overlay write
(temp-file + `os.replace`, mirroring `prompt_authoring._atomic_write`). If the overlay write fails,
the orphaned `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` is harmless (a prompt with no active routing never runs). The
overlay write itself is atomic (no half-written JSON), and adding the id to `activate` is part of the
SAME overlay write — so activation is all-or-nothing. No partial-active state is reachable.

## Acceptance Criteria (remediation additions)

- [ ] `POST /criterion/preview` dispatches on `exec` read directly (DET → grounding scan; LLM →
      `eval_solver.run_case` criterion arm) and returns `{verdict, finding?, rationale}` for BOTH an
      LLM criterion and a DET invariant; container/ISF → a clear 4xx.
- [ ] Routing-fields authoring writes the prompt then a single ATOMIC overlay write (temp+replace);
      a failed overlay write leaves no active criterion (activation requires the `activate` entry,
      written atomically with the routing entry).


## DET preview materializes the fixture (remediation, run 2)

`engine_b.scan` scans a repo FILESYSTEM, not an inline string — so the DET-criterion preview cannot
scan `fixture.input` directly. It materializes the fixture into a DISPOSABLE temp dir (reusing the
`eval_solver.case_store` pattern: `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory` + git init), writes `fixture.input`
to a file whose name is `fixture.filename` (a new optional fixture field) — defaulting to a name the
detector's `languages`/`file_globs` accept (e.g. `preview.py` for a Python-scoped rule; a generic
`preview.txt` when unconstrained) — then runs `engine_b.scan(tmpdir, registry=<the criterion's
detector slice>)`. Fire ⇔ a `match` record on that file; an `abstain` (tool absent) → reported per
`fail_mode` (open → no-fire/skip, closed → a "coverage gap" no-fire note), never a crash. The temp
dir is removed on exit. (The LLM preview needs no materialization — `pass1_chunk` takes inline
text.) So the endpoint's DET arm = materialize→scan; its LLM arm = `run_case` over inline text.

- [ ] The DET preview materializes `fixture.input` into a temp file (named via `fixture.filename` or
      inferred from the detector's language/globs), scans it with the criterion's detector slice, and
      maps match→fire / abstain→per-`fail_mode`; a test asserts a planted violation fires and a clean
      fixture does not.
compiled_state.title: Visual editor: author project criteria + live must-fire/must-not-fire preview endpoint
